name: healthcare_newsroom_s_automated_moderation_lumen_st_lt
premise: A hospital incident command bridge convened after a critical infrastructure or patient-flow failure with competing clinical priorities. Bed capacity, consent, and chart documentation are all under real-time scrutiny. The situation maps to internal runbooks and external messaging tied to: healthcare newsroom s automated moderation lumen st lt. The moral tension is explicitly framed as short term versus long term: leadership cannot fully satisfy both without accepting tradeoffs. Insurance and risk are insisting on a paper trail before any irreversible action; operations says delay costs measurable harm. Telemetry and logs are diverging; the SOC lead will freeze writes in twelve minutes if no accountable owner signs off. Any exception must be time-boxed, named, and reversible where technically feasible. Anyone affected by an automated hold or model output must retain a human escalation path that is actually staffed.

agents.name: Kaito Dubois
agents.identity: I am Kaito Dubois, the accountable decision-maker on this Healthcare bridge; I own what gets logged, communicated, and executed in the next window.
agents.goal: I need to stop the bleeding without baking in a rule that later punishes long term whenever someone invokes short term.

agents.name: Marco Berg
agents.identity: I am Marco Berg, responsible for surfacing who gets protected last when systems fail; I am watching for silent harm and unfair distribution.
agents.goal: I want safeguards so long term is not sacrificed by default just because short term feels morally loud in the moment.

choices: I will choose a narrow, time-boxed authorization with explicit human override, subgroup monitoring, and a hard stop if harm indicators rise—accepting slower relief to protect long term.

I will choose a broad immediate authorization to stabilize outcomes fast, with transparent metrics and rapid correction afterward—accepting higher risk to long term to secure short term before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: External parties will infer a decision if none is posted. With partial evidence, Kaito Dubois must decide under short term versus long term. What does Kaito Dubois do?
